claims.guidance: After calling the character-profile load function (e.g. AuraProfile.LoadCharacterSettings), validate every expected sub-key and replace nil values with their defaults before using them. Guard at least: RuntimeAuras (init to {}), RuntimeSettings (copy DefaultSettings), Enabled (copy DefaultConfiguration.Enabled), Debug (copy DefaultConfiguration.Debug). This is the correct boundary — not inside the library.
claims.statement: WAR's SavedVariables engine persists partial or malformed config tables across sessions. If a previous version of an addon wrote an incomplete table (missing expected sub-keys), the next version receives the partial table from LoadCharacterSettings and all missing keys are nil — even though the top-level character entry exists. Nil checks on the entry itself are insufficient.

claims.guidance: Add an explicit `if table == nil then return end` (or equivalent early-return) before every pairs()/ipairs() call site that touches config data loaded from SavedVariables. Affected patterns in Aura: PrepareSettingsForRuntime, CleanInternalMembers (ipairs over RuntimeAuras), CreateListDisplayData (pairs over RuntimeAuras).
claims.statement: Any Lua function that iterates a WAR addon config table with pairs() or ipairs() must guard the table for nil before the call, even if the table was initialized earlier. SavedVariables corruption or partial-load scenarios mean the table can be nil at any call site that runs before a full validation pass.

claims.guidance: In any AuraShares loop over the per-character config table, check both the entry and the Auras sub-table before iterating. Failure to guard data.Auras produces a `attempt to call a nil value (pairs)` crash at runtime.
claims.statement: AuraShares iterates AuraAddon.Configuration for all characters and accesses data.Auras. Not all character entries are guaranteed to have an Auras sub-table — entries written by a broken or partial implementation will have a nil Auras key. The guard must be: `if( data ~= nil and data.Auras ~= nil )` — not just `if( data ~= nil )`.

confidence.rationale: All errors were reproduced at runtime by the user and resolved immediately by the nil guards described. Root cause (SavedVariables written by broken code persisting partial keys) is a well-known WAR addon pattern.

evidence.summary: At game load, AuraSettings.lua:114 crashed with 'pairs(nil)' because RuntimeAuras was nil. Root cause: AuraProfile.LoadCharacterSettings returned a config entry missing the Auras key (written by old broken code). Fix: after OnLoad assignment, guard `if AuraAddon.RuntimeAuras == nil then AuraAddon.RuntimeAuras = {} end`.

ButtonSetPressedFlag was called with nil as second argument because RuntimeConfiguration.Enabled and .Debug were nil. Same root cause (partial SavedVariables). Fix: guard both fields falling back to DefaultConfiguration values immediately after OnLoad assignment.

AuraAddon.lua:200 crashed with 'ipairs(nil)' in PrepareSettingsForRuntime because RuntimeAuras was nil at that call site. Fix: early-return guard at the top of PrepareSettingsForRuntime and CleanInternalMembers.
AuraSettings.lua CreateListDisplayData crashed with 'pairs(nil)'. Same pattern as PrepareSettingsForRuntime. All config-iterating functions need nil guards on RuntimeAuras.

AuraShares.lua:135 crashed with 'pairs(nil)' because data.Auras was nil for a stale character entry. The existing check `if( data ~= nil )` was insufficient. Fix: added `and data.Auras ~= nil` to the condition.

criteria: The SavedVariables-partial-key pattern should be promoted as a general Lua caveat.
The AuraShares-specific claim can be scoped to Aura only.
promotion.notes: Promote as Lua conventions guidance. The SavedVariables partial-key pitfall affects any WAR addon that evolves its data schema across versions. This is a broadly applicable defensive coding pattern.

source.context: Aura profile feature was implemented and tested live. Adding nil guards at the load boundary and at every pairs/ipairs call site resolved all errors.

claims.guidance: Never use an EditBox element as the relativeTo target in a sibling's Anchor element. Always anchor sibling controls directly to a known stable container ($parent, $parentButtonBackground, or the main window) with explicit absolute x offsets. The root cause is that the internal layout frame of EA_EditBox_DefaultFrame does not expose its outer bounds as a reliable anchor point.
claims.statement: EA_EditBox_DefaultFrame elements cannot be reliably used as relativeTo anchor references for sibling controls. Chaining a control's anchor off 'topright of EA_EditBox_DefaultFrame' resolves to wrong or negative x coordinates at runtime.

claims.guidance: When adding a row to the footer ButtonBackground, increase BOTH $parentButtonBackground Size y AND the main window's Size y by the same pixel amount. Example: adding a 35px profile row requires adding 35px to the main window height. Failing to do so compresses the ListBox rather than expanding the window.
claims.statement: EA_Window_DefaultButtonBottomFrame auto-anchors to the parent window's bottom edge. Increasing its Size y (to add a row) does NOT grow the parent window — it compresses the content ListBox above it instead. confidence.rationale: BOM-caused parse error observed at runtime with exact error message. Removal of BOM fixed the error. Definitive negative test.

claims.guidance: Never call AuraAddon.CleanInternalMembers() while the engine is running unless you immediately follow it with AuraEngine.Stop() + AuraEngine.Start() (as SwitchProfile correctly does). claims.statement: To safely snapshot RuntimeAuras for persistence (without affecting the live engine), copy with AuraHelpers.CopyTable(), then iterate the copy and remove keys matching '^internal.*' from each aura's Data sub-table. Use a two-pass approach (collect keys in one table, then remove) to avoid modifying the table while iterating it.

confidence.rationale: Bug reproduced in-game (icons disappeared after profile save), root cause traced through the call chain, fix confirmed working by user.

claims.guidance: After calling the character-profile load function (e.g. AuraProfile.LoadCharacterSettings), validate every expected sub-key and replace nil values with their defaults before using them. Guard at least: RuntimeAuras (init to {}), RuntimeSettings (copy DefaultSettings), Enabled (copy DefaultConfiguration.Enabled), Debug (copy DefaultConfiguration.Debug). This is the correct boundary — not inside the library.
claims.statement: WAR's SavedVariables engine persists partial or malformed config tables across sessions. If a previous version of an addon wrote an incomplete table (missing expected sub-keys), the next version receives the partial table from LoadCharacterSettings and all missing keys are nil — even though the top-level character entry exists. Nil checks on the entry itself are insufficient.

claims.guidance: Add an explicit `if table == nil then return end` (or equivalent early-return) before every pairs()/ipairs() call site that touches config data loaded from SavedVariables. Affected patterns in Aura: PrepareSettingsForRuntime, CleanInternalMembers (ipairs over RuntimeAuras), CreateListDisplayData (pairs over RuntimeAuras).
claims.statement: Any Lua function that iterates a WAR addon config table with pairs() or ipairs() must guard the table for nil before the call, even if the table was initialized earlier. SavedVariables corruption or partial-load scenarios mean the table can be nil at any call site that runs before a full validation pass.

claims.guidance: In any AuraShares loop over the per-character config table, check both the entry and the Auras sub-table before iterating. Failure to guard data.Auras produces a `attempt to call a nil value (pairs)` crash at runtime.
claims.statement: AuraShares iterates AuraAddon.Configuration for all characters and accesses data.Auras. Not all character entries are guaranteed to have an Auras sub-table — entries written by a broken or partial implementation will have a nil Auras key. The guard must be: `if( data ~= nil and data.Auras ~= nil )` — not just `if( data ~= nil )`.

confidence.rationale: All errors were reproduced at runtime by the user and resolved immediately by the nil guards described. Root cause (SavedVariables written by broken code persisting partial keys) is a well-known WAR addon pattern.

evidence.summary: At game load, AuraSettings.lua:114 crashed with 'pairs(nil)' because RuntimeAuras was nil. Root cause: AuraProfile.LoadCharacterSettings returned a config entry missing the Auras key (written by old broken code). Fix: after OnLoad assignment, guard `if AuraAddon.RuntimeAuras == nil then AuraAddon.RuntimeAuras = {} end`.

ButtonSetPressedFlag was called with nil as second argument because RuntimeConfiguration.Enabled and .Debug were nil. Same root cause (partial SavedVariables). Fix: guard both fields falling back to DefaultConfiguration values immediately after OnLoad assignment.

AuraAddon.lua:200 crashed with 'ipairs(nil)' in PrepareSettingsForRuntime because RuntimeAuras was nil at that call site. Fix: early-return guard at the top of PrepareSettingsForRuntime and CleanInternalMembers.
AuraSettings.lua CreateListDisplayData crashed with 'pairs(nil)'. Same pattern as PrepareSettingsForRuntime. All config-iterating functions need nil guards on RuntimeAuras.

AuraShares.lua:135 crashed with 'pairs(nil)' because data.Auras was nil for a stale character entry. The existing check `if( data ~= nil )` was insufficient. Fix: added `and data.Auras ~= nil` to the condition.

criteria: The SavedVariables-partial-key pattern should be promoted as a general Lua caveat.

promotion.notes: Promote as Lua conventions guidance. The SavedVariables partial-key pitfall affects any WAR addon that evolves its data schema across versions. This is a broadly applicable defensive coding pattern.

evidence.summary: After truncating APAGui.lua with Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 in PowerShell 5.1, the game logged: Error Reading Lua buffer Interface/AddOns/AdvancedPetAssist/APAGui.lua: [string ...]:1: '=' expected near '»'. Byte inspection confirmed first 3 bytes were the UTF-8 BOM. Re-saving with [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false) resolved the error immediately.

promotion.notes: Promote as a Lua conventions caveat. This is an operational pitfall when editing WAR addon files on Windows using PowerShell tools and affects any addon developer.

source.context: APAGui.lua was re-saved using PowerShell Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 during a file-repair step. The game reported a parse error on next load. Removing the BOM fixed the error immediately.

claims.guidance: Use row=1, col=1 for the heading line with COLOR_HEADING applied. Use row=2+ for body lines. The row index is 1-based. Do NOT call Tooltips.AddLineToTooltip — that function does not exist and causes a nil-call runtime error.
claims.statement: The standard pattern for a multi-line text-only tooltip is: Tooltips.CreateTextOnlyTooltip(windowName, nil) then Tooltips.SetTooltipText(row, col, wstring) for each line, Tooltips.SetTooltipColorDef(1, 1, Tooltips.COLOR_HEADING) for the heading row, then Tooltips.Finalize() and Tooltips.AnchorTooltip(Tooltips.ANCHOR_WINDOW_RIGHT). The MouseOverEnd handler should call Tooltips.ClearTooltip().

claims.guidance: Never call Tooltips.AddLineToTooltip. To add a separator or body line, call Tooltips.SetTooltipText with the next row index instead.
claims.statement: Tooltips.AddLineToTooltip does not exist in the WAR engine. Calling it produces the runtime error: attempt to call field 'AddLineToTooltip' (a nil value).

claims.guidance: Pass the table from GetAbilityData(id) as the first argument. Use Tooltips.ANCHOR_WINDOW_RIGHT as anchor. The fifth argument appears unused (nil). Corroborate in additional addons before treating as a hard contract.
claims.statement: Tooltips.CreateAbilityTooltip(abilityData, windowName, anchor, hintWstring, nil) creates an ability tooltip anchored to a named window. The first argument is the table returned by GetAbilityData(id).

confidence.rationale: CreateTextOnlyTooltip + SetTooltipText + Finalize + AnchorTooltip pattern rendered correctly in-game and is user-validated. AddLineToTooltip nil error is a hard runtime fact.

evidence.summary: Tooltips.AddLineToTooltip raised a nil-call error at runtime in AdvancedPetAssist during cast blacklist panel implementation. After removing it, the CreateTextOnlyTooltip + SetTooltipText + Finalize + AnchorTooltip pattern rendered correctly in-game on hover. User confirmed the entire blacklist UI works as intended.

criteria: Corroborate Tooltips.CreateAbilityTooltip signature in additional addons before upgrading to HIGH.
Retain HIGH confidence for the negative AddLineToTooltip fact — runtime error is definitive.
promotion.notes: Promote to war_api_facts.md. The Tooltips namespace is entirely absent from docs/war-api. These are the first documented facts about it.

source.context: Drag-and-drop cast blacklist UI in AdvancedPetAssist. Hover tooltips on the drop slot and ability rows were exercised in-game. User confirmed both UI and behavior work as intended. Tooltips.AddLineToTooltip nil error was observed in a prior iteration, confirming that function does not exist.

claims.guidance: Never call AuraAddon.CleanInternalMembers() while the engine is running unless you immediately follow it with AuraEngine.Stop() + AuraEngine.Start() (as SwitchProfile correctly does). For snapshot/save operations that do not switch profiles, copy RuntimeAuras with AuraHelpers.CopyTable() and strip internal-* keys from the copy only — do not touch the live runtime table.
claims.statement: AuraAddon.CleanInternalMembers() iterates RuntimeAuras and calls Aura:CleanInternalMembers() on each, which destroys all runtime overlay windows (internal-runtimewindowid, internal-runtimetimerwindowid, internal-runtimeflashwindowid) and clears their IDs from aura.Data. If the engine is still running when this is called, the windows are gone but engineRunning remains true, so AuraEngine.Start() becomes a no-op and the windows are never rebuilt. Aura icons stop displaying.

claims.guidance: Pattern: `local copy = AuraHelpers.CopyTable(AuraAddon.RuntimeAuras)` then for each entry `for _, auraCopy in ipairs(copy) do -- collect internal keys, then nil them in auraCopy.Data end`. This is the correct substitute for calling CleanInternalMembers() before a CopyTable when the engine must remain running.
claims.statement: To safely snapshot RuntimeAuras for persistence (without affecting the live engine), copy with AuraHelpers.CopyTable(), then iterate the copy and remove keys matching '^internal.*' from each aura's Data sub-table. Use a two-pass approach (collect keys in one table, then remove) to avoid modifying the table while iterating it.

confidence.rationale: Bug reproduced in-game (icons disappeared after profile save), root cause traced through the call chain, fix confirmed working by user.

evidence.summary: After calling SaveCurrentAsProfile (which called CleanInternalMembers), all Aura overlay icons stopped appearing. The engine was still flagged as running so Start() was skipped on settings close. Root cause confirmed by tracing CleanInternalMembers -> Aura:CleanInternalMembers -> DeleteWindow on all internal-runtimewindowid entries.
Replaced CleanInternalMembers() + CopyTable() with CopyTable() + two-pass internal key strip on the copy only. User confirmed aura icons continued displaying after saving a profile.

criteria: HIGH confidence — directly user-validated at runtime.
Promote both claims to lua_conventions.md.
promotion.notes: Promote as a Lua lifecycle caveat. The pattern of CleanInternalMembers being called without a matching Stop/Start is a latent bug that could affect any future profile or snapshot feature in Aura. The safe snapshot pattern is reusable.

source.context: Aura profile Save feature caused aura overlay icons to stop displaying after saving a profile. Root cause: SaveCurrentAsProfile called CleanInternalMembers() while the engine was running. CleanInternalMembers destroys all runtime overlay windows and clears their IDs. AuraEngine.Start() was then a no-op because engineRunning was still true. Fix: copy RuntimeAuras with CopyTable and strip internal-* keys from the copy only, leaving live engine state untouched.

claims.guidance: After calling the character-profile load function (e.g. AuraProfile.LoadCharacterSettings), validate every expected sub-key and replace nil values with their defaults before using them. Guard at least: RuntimeAuras (init to {}), RuntimeSettings (copy DefaultSettings), Enabled (copy DefaultConfiguration.Enabled), Debug (copy DefaultConfiguration.Debug). This is the correct boundary — not inside the library.
claims.statement: WAR's SavedVariables engine persists partial or malformed config tables across sessions. If a previous version of an addon wrote an incomplete table (missing expected sub-keys), the next version receives the partial table from LoadCharacterSettings and all missing keys are nil — even though the top-level character entry exists. Nil checks on the entry itself are insufficient.

claims.guidance: Add an explicit `if table == nil then return end` (or equivalent early-return) before every pairs()/ipairs() call site that touches config data loaded from SavedVariables. Affected patterns in Aura: PrepareSettingsForRuntime, CleanInternalMembers (ipairs over RuntimeAuras), CreateListDisplayData (pairs over RuntimeAuras).
claims.statement: Any Lua function that iterates a WAR addon config table with pairs() or ipairs() must guard the table for nil before the call, even if the table was initialized earlier. SavedVariables corruption or partial-load scenarios mean the table can be nil at any call site that runs before a full validation pass.

claims.guidance: In any AuraShares loop over the per-character config table, check both the entry and the Auras sub-table before iterating. Failure to guard data.Auras produces a `attempt to call a nil value (pairs)` crash at runtime.
claims.statement: AuraShares iterates AuraAddon.Configuration for all characters and accesses data.Auras. Not all character entries are guaranteed to have an Auras sub-table — entries written by a broken or partial implementation will have a nil Auras key. The guard must be: `if( data ~= nil and data.Auras ~= nil )` — not just `if( data ~= nil )`.

confidence.rationale: All errors were reproduced at runtime by the user and resolved immediately by the nil guards described. Root cause (SavedVariables written by broken code persisting partial keys) is a well-known WAR addon pattern.

evidence.summary: At game load, AuraSettings.lua:114 crashed with 'pairs(nil)' because RuntimeAuras was nil. Root cause: AuraProfile.LoadCharacterSettings returned a config entry missing the Auras key (written by old broken code). Fix: after OnLoad assignment, guard `if AuraAddon.RuntimeAuras == nil then AuraAddon.RuntimeAuras = {} end`.

ButtonSetPressedFlag was called with nil as second argument because RuntimeConfiguration.Enabled and .Debug were nil. Same root cause (partial SavedVariables). Fix: guard both fields falling back to DefaultConfiguration values immediately after OnLoad assignment.

AuraAddon.lua:200 crashed with 'ipairs(nil)' in PrepareSettingsForRuntime because RuntimeAuras was nil at that call site. Fix: early-return guard at the top of PrepareSettingsForRuntime and CleanInternalMembers.
AuraSettings.lua CreateListDisplayData crashed with 'pairs(nil)'. Same pattern as PrepareSettingsForRuntime. All config-iterating functions need nil guards on RuntimeAuras.

AuraShares.lua:135 crashed with 'pairs(nil)' because data.Auras was nil for a stale character entry. The existing check `if( data ~= nil )` was insufficient. Fix: added `and data.Auras ~= nil` to the condition.

criteria: The SavedVariables-partial-key pattern should be promoted as a general Lua caveat.
The AuraShares-specific claim can be scoped to Aura only.
promotion.notes: Promote as Lua conventions guidance. The SavedVariables partial-key pitfall affects any WAR addon that evolves its data schema across versions. This is a broadly applicable defensive coding pattern.

source.context: Aura profile feature was implemented and tested live. Old SavedVariables written by a partial/broken previous implementation caused cascading nil errors at OnLoad time: RuntimeConfiguration.Auras, .Enabled, and .Debug were all nil after LoadCharacterSettings returned, and AuraShares crashed iterating data.Auras. Adding nil guards at the load boundary and at every pairs/ipairs call site resolved all errors.

claims.guidance: Set draganddrop="true" on the Button element in XML. In the OnLButtonUp Lua handler, check Cursor.IconOnCursor() and guard on Cursor.Data.Source before reading the payload. Ensure handleinput="true" or handleinput is not explicitly disabled on the button and its parent chain.
claims.statement: Adding draganddrop="true" to an XML Button element enables it to receive drag-and-drop events. The OnLButtonUp handler fires when the player releases a dragged item or ability over the button. Without this attribute the button does not receive drop events.

confidence.rationale: draganddrop attribute on Button exercised at runtime. Drop events (OnLButtonUp) fired correctly when attribute was present. User confirmed the drag-and-drop feature works end-to-end.

evidence.summary: APA_FTDropSlotTemplate Button declared with draganddrop="true" received ability drops from the action bar in AdvancedPetAssist. The OnLButtonUp handler fired and the ability ID was correctly captured via Cursor.Data.ObjectId. User confirmed the drag-and-drop cast blacklist feature works.

criteria: Validate whether non-Button element types also support draganddrop if evidence is found in other addons.
promotion.notes: The draganddrop XML attribute is not documented anywhere in docs/war-api or seed files. This is the first documented fact about it.

source.context: APA_FTDropSlotTemplate XML Button with draganddrop=true used in cast blacklist drop slot. User successfully dropped abilities onto the button in-game.

claims.guidance: Never use an EditBox element as the relativeTo target in a sibling's Anchor element. Always anchor sibling controls directly to a known stable container ($parent, $parentButtonBackground, or the main window) with explicit absolute x offsets. The root cause is that the internal layout frame of EA_EditBox_DefaultFrame does not expose its outer bounds as a reliable anchor point.
claims.statement: EA_EditBox_DefaultFrame elements cannot be reliably used as relativeTo anchor references for sibling controls. Chaining a control's anchor off 'topright of EA_EditBox_DefaultFrame' resolves to wrong or negative x coordinates at runtime.

claims.guidance: When adding a row to the footer ButtonBackground, increase BOTH $parentButtonBackground Size y AND the main window's Size y by the same pixel amount. Example: adding a 35px profile row requires adding 35px to the main window height. Failing to do so compresses the ListBox rather than expanding the window.
claims.statement: EA_Window_DefaultButtonBottomFrame auto-anchors to the parent window's bottom edge. Increasing its Size y (to add a row) does NOT grow the parent window — it compresses the content ListBox above it instead. The parent window Size y must be increased by the same amount as the footer height increase to preserve the ListBox area.

confidence.rationale: Directly observed at runtime by user. EditBox-as-anchor produced reproducible wrong x coordinates; plain $parent-Based anchors with explicit offsets fixed the layout immediately. The window+footer coupling was confirmed by user-side manual correction.

evidence.summary: In AuraSettings.xml, a Save button anchored 'topright' of $parentProfileNameEdit (an EA_EditBox_DefaultFrame) appeared off-screen to the left. Replacing the anchor with an explicit x offset from $parentButtonBackground topleft rendered the button in the correct column.

Increasing $parentButtonBackground Size y from 120 to 155 without changing the main window Size y caused the aura ListBox to shrink by 35px. User manually corrected the main window Size y to 510 (+35 from the original 475) to restore the ListBox height.

criteria: If corroborated in other addons, raise EditBox anchor caveat to DEFINITIVE.
Footer/window-y coupling is a direct consequence of EA_Window_DefaultButtonBottomFrame anchor semantics and can be promoted immediately.
promotion.notes: Promote as XML layout caveats. Both behaviors are reliable pitfalls that affect any addon adding rows to a footer or using EditBox elements as sibling anchors.

source.context: Aura profile controls row added to AuraSettings.xml footer. Controls chaining anchors through an EditBox rendered off-screen. Re-anchoring all controls directly to $parentButtonBackground with explicit x offsets resolved the layout. Discovered separately that increasing ButtonBG Size y without also increasing the main window Size y compressed the ListBox instead of growing the window.

claims.guidance: If a control renders in the wrong column or outside the window, clear its anchors and re-anchor it from `$parent` or a stable container such as `$parentButtonBackground` with explicit offsets.
claims.statement: When footer controls or edit boxes drift under sibling-based anchoring, anchoring them directly to a stable parent container is more reliable than chaining them through neighboring controls.

claims.guidance: Use `LabelGetTextDimensions()` and then resize or re-anchor neighboring labels and edit boxes to avoid truncation, overlap, or misplaced values.
claims.statement: Text-driven header or form rows are more stable when label dimensions are measured after LabelSetText and adjacent controls are positioned from the measured width.

claims.guidance: If alternating row tint stops short, widen the row template window and its child label widths instead of only changing the ListBox anchors.
claims.statement: A ListBox row highlight or tint only spans the width of the row template; widening the list box alone does not widen the tinted row background.

claims.guidance: Prefer keeping button height around 39px and increase the containing button-background height before squeezing stacked rows closer together.
claims.statement: Default resizable WAR buttons render more cleanly at their native full height and need adequate footer/container height to prevent stacked controls from overlapping.

confidence.rationale: Observed across multiple UI fixes in Aura and validated in game by the user, but still best treated as platform guidance rather than a hard engine contract.

evidence.summary: Aura profile controls stopped drifting only after edit boxes and value labels were re-anchored directly to stable parent windows and footer containers.
Aura used LabelGetTextDimensions with runtime WindowSetDimensions and re-anchoring to keep `Active Profile:` and `Profile Name:` rows readable during in-game validation.
The profile list row tint did not span the full width until the row template and row label widths were widened, even though the ListBox itself was already wider.
Stacked footer buttons in Aura overlapped or felt cramped until the button background height was increased and full-height button sizing was preserved during in-game review.

criteria: Keep MEDIUM confidence unless corroborated by more addons or canonical platform documentation.
Treat these as practical layout patterns for WAR XML UI work.

claims.guidance: Treat the table attribute as the backing collection for list rows, and rebuild that Lua table before refreshing or reordering the list.
claims.statement: ListData appears to bind a ListBox to a named Lua table through its table attribute.

claims.guidance: Use windowname and variable to bind text-bearing row children from structured table entries, and keep format declarations conservative.
claims.statement: ListColumn entries under ListData appear to map fields from each backing-table entry onto row-template child windows.

claims.guidance: Use ListColumns for straightforward text binding, and reserve populationfunction for extra row work such as icons or row-specific state that is not expressed directly in XML.
claims.statement: When populationfunction is present, Lua can perform custom row population and use PopulatorIndices plus ListBox APIs to manage visible-row ordering and row-to-data mapping.

claims.guidance: If the list only needs ListColumns text binding, an empty populationfunction may be enough; add a Lua population function only when the row requires extra runtime setup.
claims.statement: A blank populationfunction appears to be accepted when XML column binding alone is sufficient for the list.

confidence.rationale: Observed repeatedly in addon implementations, but ListData itself is not yet covered by canonical platform documentation.
